methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Transportation (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 5 same-sector peers exist in Transportation, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Einride
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Harbinger
private.rationale: Same sector (Transportation), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Candela is a Series C-stage transportation company that has raised €129M+ to date. The transportation sector comp universe comprises seven private peers including Einride, Harbinger, Rivian, The Boring Company, and REGENT Craft, with no public comparables available for direct benchmarking.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this analysis. The identified peer set contains only two same-stage comparable companies and zero adjacent-stage or similar-tier datapoints—below the three-datapoint minimum required for statistical reliability. While five transportation-sector peers exist broadly, narrowing to stage-appropriate and valuation-relevant matches yields insufficient density. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable category drift and obscure sector-specific dynamics.

This analysis cannot proceed to a triangulated valuation range without additional comparable transaction data, revenue multiples, or margin benchmarks within the transportation frontier-tech segment. The key uncertainty is the scarcity of sufficiently analogous, recent funding events or public market comparables at Candela's development stage and capital intensity. Future valuation work would require either: (i) access to contemporaneous Series C or Series B/D rounds among the identified peers; (ii) revenue or EBITDA multiples from later-stage or public transportation technology companies; or (iii) DCF modeling with explicit market-sizing and margin assumptions. Without such inputs, peer-based valuation triangulation cannot be responsibly executed.
